assumptions: A cloud provider account (AWS/GCP/Azure/Scaleway) can be connected.
A Git repository or container image is available to deploy.

developer_goal: Create a Qovery account, provision a managed cloud cluster, and deploy a first application until it is reachable.

first_success.outcome: After cluster creation, a first application deploy completes and the app is reachable via the Qovery-provided URL/endpoint.
first_success.observable_signal: Application status shows running/deployed and the public URL loads application content.

route_selection.surface: Qovery Console production path: cloud cluster + first application
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: Qovery Console production path: cloud cluster + first application

candidates.name: Qovery Console production path: cloud cluster + first application
candidates.reason: Qovery Get Started presents hosted Console/cloud paths on the customer cloud. Prefer Console + managed cloud cluster + deploy first application over the free local k3s learning path, which skips real cloud onboarding.

required_path.action: Sign up for Qovery and open the Console.
Create a managed Kubernetes cluster on a chosen cloud provider and wait until ready.
Create a project/environment and add a first application from Git or container image.
Open the application URL and confirm it loads.

prerequisites.description: Qovery account (free start, no credit card per Get Started).
Cloud provider account credentials for cluster creation (~30 minutes).

uncertainties.question: Are all signup and first-success fields for Qovery fully enumerated in public docs?
uncertainties.impact: Atomic field counts inside account or billing steps may be incomplete.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Live console fields can differ from documentation screenshots.
